chief_complaint: A 45-year-old female presented with sudden onset left-sided weakness and difficulty speaking.
history: The patient has a history of cardiac myxoma, which was diagnosed 2 years ago. She has been experiencing episodes of palpitations and shortness of breath. She has no history of hypertension, diabetes, or smoking. She takes aspirin for her cardiac condition.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination revealed left-sided weakness and dysarthria.
labs: ECG: normal sinus rhythm with a left atrial enlargement. Bloods: normal complete blood count and basic metabolic panel. Imaging: CT head showed an ischemic stroke in the left middle cerebral artery territory. Echocardiography confirmed the presence of a cardiac myxoma.
correct_diagnosis: Ischemic stroke due to cardiac myxoma
differentials: Ischemic stroke due to cardiac myxoma
correct_ranking: Ischemic stroke due to cardiac myxoma
